title: Java 17–21 Features
summary: Records, sealed classes, pattern matching, text blocks, virtual threads (Project Loom), and structured concurrency.

sections.title: Sealed Class Hierarchy
sections.content:
graph TD
    Shape["sealed interface Shape\npermits Circle, Rectangle, Triangle"] --> Circle["record Circle\nimplements Shape"]
    Shape --> Rectangle["record Rectangle\nimplements Shape"]
    Shape --> Triangle["record Triangle\nimplements Shape"]
    X["class Hexagon"] -->|❌ not permitted| Shape
    style Shape fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style X fill:#dc2626,color:#fff

sections.title: Virtual Threads vs Platform Threads
sections.content:
graph TB
    subgraph Platform Threads
        PT1[Platform Thread 1\n~1-2MB] 
        PT2[Platform Thread 2\n~1-2MB]
        PT3[Platform Thread N\n~1-2MB]
        OS[OS Kernel Threads]
        PT1 --> OS
        PT2 --> OS
        PT3 --> OS
    end
    subgraph Virtual Threads - Project Loom
        VT1[VThread 1] 
        VT2[VThread 2]
        VTMM[VThread 1,000,000]
        Carrier1[Carrier Thread 1]
        Carrier2[Carrier Thread 2]
        VT1 --> Carrier1
        VT2 --> Carrier2
        VTMM --> Carrier1
        Carrier1 --> OS2[OS Kernel Threads]
        Carrier2 --> OS2
    end
    style Carrier1 fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Carrier2 fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff

sections.title: Spring Boot 3.2+ supports virtual threads out of the box
sections.content: Set spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true in application.properties to have Spring Boot use virtual threads for web request handling. This dramatically improves I/O-bound throughput without any code changes.

sections.question: What is the key advantage of sealed classes combined with pattern matching?
options: They allow classes to have multiple inheritance
They enable exhaustive switch expressions — the compiler knows all possible subtypes
They improve runtime performance by reducing instanceof checks
sections.explanation: Sealed classes declare exactly which classes can implement/extend them. Combined with pattern matching in switch, the compiler can verify exhaustiveness — if you handle all permitted subtypes, no default case is needed and the compiler warns if you miss one.
